title: Roommate Arbitrage
premise: Your roommate Alex's girlfriend, Sam, has been staying over every night for the past two months — using your shower, eating your food, and cranking the thermostat. The lease, utilities, and groceries are still split 50-50. You need to renegotiate a fairer arrangement without blowing up a friendship that's been solid for three years.
setting: A two-bedroom apartment in a mid-sized city, current month. The living room is a shared space with worn-out couches, a coffee table covered in coasters and remote controls, and a small dining table cluttered with takeout menus.
goal: Reach a new rent-split and household agreement (e.g., 40-30-30 or a fixed contribution) that all three parties accept, while maintaining a positive relationship with Alex. Failure: the agreement falls apart, Alex is offended, or you lose the friendship.
gm_notes: Complications to introduce: Sam overhears part of the conversation or feels unwelcome. Alex might get defensive about his relationship. The landlord might have a policy on additional occupants. Pacing: start with a casual mention, then escalate to a formal sit-down. Hidden info: Sam has a key she uses when Alex isn't there.

roles.name: The Leaseholder
roles.description: Original leaseholder, employed full-time, enjoys a quiet home with fair sharing.
roles.private_info: You recently noticed Sam's mail arriving at the apartment. You're also concerned about a pet policy. You want to keep things civil but feel taken advantage of.

npcs.name: Alex
npcs.description: Your long-time roommate and friend, easygoing but conflict-averse. Works in tech and often works late.
npcs.hidden_agenda: He's deeply in love with Sam and feels guilty about the situation but is afraid of losing her if he pushes for more boundaries. He secretly hopes you'll just tolerate it.

npcs.name: Sam
npcs.description: Alex's girlfriend, a freelance graphic designer with an irregular schedule. She's warm but a bit oblivious to the unwritten rules of shared living.
npcs.hidden_agenda: She actually hates her current apartment and is angling to move in full-time. She's testing the waters by gradually asserting more presence.
